---
title: Tutorials
description: Every tutorial, ordered as a learning path from the standard analysis workflow through the model engines to calibration and published replications.
toc: false
listing:
  - id: getting-started
    contents:
      - full-pipeline.qmd
      - byo-outputs.qmd
      - parameter-inputs.qmd
    type: grid
    grid-columns: 3
    fields: [title, description]
    sort: false
    categories: false
    filter-ui: false
    sort-ui: false
  - id: model-engines
    contents:
      - mdm-cohort.qmd
      - microsim.qmd
      - markov-vs-microsim.qmd
      - des.qmd
      - seir-vaccination.qmd
    type: grid
    grid-columns: 3
    fields: [title, description]
    sort: false
    categories: false
    filter-ui: false
    sort-ui: false
  - id: sensitivity-voi
    contents:
      - dsa.qmd
      - voi.qmd
    type: grid
    grid-columns: 3
    fields: [title, description]
    sort: false
    categories: false
    filter-ui: false
    sort-ui: false
  - id: calibration
    contents:
      - calibration-workflow.qmd
      - calibrate-abc.qmd
      - calibrate-sbi.qmd
      - surrogate-calibration.qmd
      - calibrate-microsim.qmd
    type: grid
    grid-columns: 3
    fields: [title, description]
    sort: false
    categories: false
    filter-ui: false
    sort-ui: false
  - id: replications
    contents:
      - replication-gallery.qmd
      - mdm-cohort-timedep.qmd
      - mdm-microsim.qmd
      - mdm-des.qmd
    type: grid
    grid-columns: 3
    fields: [title, description]
    sort: false
    categories: false
    filter-ui: false
    sort-ui: false
---

The tutorials are ordered as a learning path. Each group builds on the ones before it, and within a group the prerequisite tutorial comes first. A reader new to the package can read top to bottom; a reader who knows the workflow can jump to the group they need.

## Getting started

These tutorials cover the standard probabilistic sensitivity analysis workflow: specify parameters, sample draws, run a model, and analyze cost-effectiveness and value of information. Start here to see the pipeline every engine plugs into, then how to feed it results or parameters computed elsewhere.

:::{#getting-started}
:::

## Model engines

Each tutorial covers one model type: it builds a model, then runs the same analysis as the getting-started workflow, so the engine is the only part that changes. The cohort state-transition model comes first as the standard engine, and the microsimulation, discrete-event, and compartmental engines follow for the problems a cohort average cannot represent.

:::{#model-engines}
:::

## Sensitivity and value of information

These two analyses quantify what the uncertainty in a model means for the decision. Deterministic sensitivity analysis asks which parameters move the result, and value-of-information analysis prices resolving the remaining uncertainty. Both read the same outcomes the engines produce.

:::{#sensitivity-voi}
:::

## Calibration

These tutorials fit model parameters to observed data, then carry the fitted uncertainty into the same analysis. The calibration workflow comes first, then three inference methods on a shared model, and finally calibration of a stochastic microsimulation.

:::{#calibration}
:::

## Replications

These tutorials reproduce published cost-effectiveness analyses with `heormodel`, each matching the source's deterministic result before extending it to a probabilistic analysis. Read them to check the package against known answers and to see complete worked models.

:::{#replications}
:::
